item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: won't lie, I don't really *get* it per se, but a hijacked TV show trying to drive people to antinatalism (and…onion worship?) is disturbing enough to merit a +1 in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree re: the affected media, but I agree that turning on the television makes more sense, feels more thematically correct, than a post-it note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a pretty good read overall. The character of Mr. Death in Broadcast Example 2 reminded me of SCP-4999 a lot, which was a nice touch (even if not intended). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, this is pretty fun. +1

One nitpick though. Radiocarbon dating wouldn't work in this situation. According to Wikipedia:

[…] the oldest dates that can be reliably measured by this process date to around 50,000 years ago, although special preparation methods occasionally permit accurate analysis of older samples.

For something older than 50,000 years, some other radioisotope with a longer half life would be used instead.

E: Ayy fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty good but the entire description feels like a fairly long-winded way of saying "makes tv shows different", and the addendum feels like a long-winded way of saying "stone structure with a tv inside". It was enjoyable enough to read (and that picture of the stone thing is awesome) but ultimately I feel like this could probably be half the length it is and not lose anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's always a good day when CadaverCommander uploads an article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two things kill the article: one, the affected media ultimately doesn't feel satisfying or spooky to read about; and two, using post-it-notes is stupid. There's a television right there to display the message on! Why deliberately lean into the trope of a Note at the End instead of having the TV turn on for the first time in a quarter-million years?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehh. I agree this felt like it meant something, but I'm not convinced it actually does. It's possible I'm not getting it, though.

Novoted - I enjoyed the general feel of this, but it doesn't seem to come together in the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yesssssss

I have no idea how you did it; corrupted media is usually a pretty hard sell for me, but you did a good job at making it feel like it means something rather than just bog standard spoopy word salad randomness. The new ending is also really great.

+1 🖤

item.title: dang picturebox all fulla snow and whatnot
item.content:encoded:
i wrote an scp that you can read. please do not submerge this scp in water for any amount of time. please do not leave this scp in direct sunlight. please do not insert this scp into anything not specifically designed to accommodate it.

have a nice day, everyone ♥

image credit
image credit

second image here, in the public domain:
https://pxhere.com/en/photo/77869
first image is original, photo taken by me.
